actions-list.actionItem: Pursuant to the order of Thursday, May 28, 2026,  the Journal of the proceedings of the Senate was deemed approved to date.
Pursuant to the order of Thursday, May 28, 2026, the morning hour being deemed expired, and the times for the recognition of the two leaders being reserved.
The following transactions of morning business occurred on today.

S.Res. 727 (Sen. Fischer):
        A resolution supporting the designation of May 29, 2026, as "Mental Health Awareness in Agriculture Day" to raise awareness around mental health in the agricultural industry and workforce and to continue to reduce stigma associated with mental illness.

        -- Senate Committee on the Judiciary discharged by Unanimous Consent.

        -- Resolution agreed to in Senate without amendment and with a preamble by Unanimous Consent.


 S.Res. 755 (Sen. Peters):
        A resolution honoring the life of the Honorable Donald W. Riegle, Jr., former Senator for the State of Michigan.

        -- Submitted in the Senate, considered, and agreed to without amendment and with a preamble by Unanimous Consent.

PN851-3:
        Kathleen S. Lane, of Montana, to be United States District Judge for the District of Montana

        -- By unanimous consent agreement, mandatory quorum required under Rule XXII waived.

        -- Cloture invoked in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 50 - 44. Record Vote Number: 131 


        -- Considered by Senate.

        -- By unanimous consent agreement, debate and vote 6/2/2026.
By unanimous consent, on the request of Mr. Thune, at  6:27 p.m., the Senate adjourned, under its order of today, until 10 a.m. tomorrow.
